name: OPEC+
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **OPEC+ PRODUCTION CUT MAINTENANCE CONTINUES THROUGH MAY — ~$2-3 CONTRIBUTION TO CURRENT BRENT FLOOR INDEPENDENT OF HORMUZ.** Brent close $101.73 May 8 (+1.66% 1D, intraday range $96-104 = trinary-regime signature). Goldman Q2 $90 target now stretched against Lloyd's List zero-transit print May 7 + 1,500-vessel Persian Gulf standstill + CENTCOM 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech — likely revises higher if trinary regime extends past May 21. World Bank $86 average for 2026 still operative in baseline. Saudi Tadawul +0.7% May 8 (treated cautiously — Friday partial trading; Sunday open will be first reaction to Saudi Al-Hadath denial of US offensive-airspace lift). UAE OPEC exit effective May 1; UAE 500K-1M bpd unconstrained capacity medium-term-BEARISH (12-24mo) for Brent (Atlantic Council). WoodMac: ADNOC Murban discount potentially widening to $3.50/bbl by Q3. **June 7 OPEC+ ministerial remains the structural test** for substantive Saudi response post-UAE-exit. With UAE OUT, post-OPEC architecture rests on Saudi-Russia-Iraq-Kuwait. **Critical contradiction in MOU framework:** if Iran ships out HEU and US lifts sanctions per MOU provisions, eventual Iranian crude return to global market adds medium-term bearish factor on top of UAE exit + forecast cuts. PRIOR FRAMING (preserved): MBS Day 9+ silence on UAE OPEC exit; Tadawul peace-dividend bearish signal for war's primary fiscal beneficiary; Saudi break-even ~$95/bbl threatens fiscal runway.

current.saudi_position: carrying more burden post-UAE; MBS publicly silent; needs higher revenue post-Sadara damage
